Pre-condition: Auth service is running on port 9000
Test Steps:
1. Send GET /health
2. Inspect response status and body

Pre-condition: Auth service running; email does not exist in DB
Test Steps:
1. Generate unique email (uuid)
2. POST /auth/register with full valid payload
3. Inspect response
Test Data: email: api_test_<uuid>@luxe-test.dev | password: TestPass@99 | first_name: ApiTest | last_name: User | phone: +10000000000
Expected Result: Status 201 | Response contains access_token (non-empty) | token_type = 'bearer' | user object with email, first_name, last_name, id

Pre-condition: A user with the test email is already registered
Test Steps:
1. POST /auth/register using an already-registered email
2. Inspect response
Test Data: email: (existing registered email) | password: TestPass@99 | first_name: ApiTest | last_name: User

Test Steps:
1. POST /auth/register with email field omitted
2. Inspect response
Test Data: password: TestPass@99 | first_name: ApiTest | last_name: User

Test Steps:
1. POST /auth/register with password field omitted
2. Inspect response
Test Data: email: new_user@luxe-test.dev | first_name: ApiTest | last_name: User

Test Steps:
1. POST /auth/register with first_name field omitted
2. Inspect response
Test Data: email: new_user@luxe-test.dev | password: TestPass@99 | last_name: User

Test Steps:
1. POST /auth/register with malformed email
2. Inspect response
Test Data: email: not-an-email | password: TestPass@99 | first_name: ApiTest | last_name: User

Test Steps:
1. POST /auth/login with valid credentials
2. Inspect response
Expected Result: Status 200 | access_token is non-empty string | user.email matches registered email

Test Steps:
1. POST /auth/login with correct email but wrong password
2. Inspect response

1. POST /auth/login with unregistered email
2. Inspect response

1. POST /auth/login with empty strings for both fields
2. Inspect response
Expected Result: Status 401 or 422 | (EmailStr rejects empty string or logic returns 401)

Pre-condition: Test user registered; valid access_token available
Test Steps:
1. GET /auth/me with Authorization: Bearer {token}
2. Inspect response
Expected Result: Status 200 | email matches registered user | id matches api_test_user['user_id']

Test Steps:
1. GET /auth/me with no Authorization header
2. Inspect response

1. GET /auth/me with malformed Bearer token
2. Inspect response

Pre-condition: Test user registered; valid token available
Test Steps:
1. PUT /auth/profile with Bearer token and updated fields
2. Inspect response

1. PUT /auth/profile with no Authorization header
2. Inspect response

Pre-condition: Airlines service is running on port 9001
Test Steps:
1. Send GET /health
2. Inspect response status and body

Pre-condition: Airlines service running; airports seeded in DB
Test Steps:
1. GET /airports
2. Inspect response
Expected Result: Status 200 | Response is a list | len >= 1 | Each item has: id, iata_code, name, city, country

Test Steps:
1. GET /airports/search?q=LON
2. Inspect response
Expected Result: Status 200 | List with >= 1 result | Each result has iata_code field

Test Steps:
1. GET /airports/search?q=ZZZNOTEXIST
2. Inspect response

1. GET /airports/search (no q param)
2. Inspect response

1. GET /airports to fetch two real iata_codes
2. GET /flights/search?origin={iata0}&destination={iata1}&date=2025-12-01&passengers=1&cabin_class=Economy
3. Inspect response shape
Test Data: origin: iata_code[0] | destination: iata_code[1] | date: 2025-12-01 | passengers: 1 | cabin_class: Economy
Expected Result: Status 200 | Response has keys: flights, total, search_params | search_params.origin matches origin (case-insensitive)

Test Steps:
1. GET /flights/search?origin=ZZZ&destination=YYY&date=2025-12-01
2. Inspect response

1. GET /flights/search?destination=LHR&date=2025-12-01 (origin omitted)
2. Inspect response

1. GET /flights/search?origin=LHR&date=2025-12-01 (destination omitted)
2. Inspect response

1. GET /flights/search?origin=LHR&destination=CDG (date omitted)
2. Inspect response

Description: Get flight by ID — not found

Pre-condition: Test user authenticated; seeded flight exists
Test Steps:
1. GET /airports — pick two iata codes
2. GET /flights/search (loop up to 5 dates) until total > 0
3. Skip test if no flights found
4. POST /bookings with auth header and flight_id
5. Inspect response
Test Data: Header: Authorization: Bearer <token> | flight_id: <first result id> | passenger_name: ApiTest User | passenger_id_number: PASS123456 | date_of_birth: 1990-01-01 | num_passengers: 1
Expected Result: Status 201 | booking_reference starts with 'LA' | status = 'confirmed' | flight_id matches requested

Pre-condition: Test user authenticated; TC-AIR-012 has run
Test Steps:
1. GET /bookings/my with Authorization header
2. Inspect response
Expected Result: Status 200 | Response is a list | At least 1 booking if TC-AIR-012 passed

Test Steps:
1. GET /bookings/{booking_id} with Authorization header
2. Inspect response

Description: Get booking by ID — no token
Test Steps:
1. GET /bookings/{booking_id} with NO Authorization header
2. Inspect response

1. PATCH /bookings/{booking_id}/cancel with Authorization header
2. Inspect response

1. PATCH /bookings/{booking_id}/cancel again (same booking)
2. Inspect response
Test Data: Header: Authorization: Bearer <token> | booking_id: (same as TC-AIR-016)

Test Steps:
1. POST /bookings with no Authorization header
2. Inspect response
Test Data: No Authorization header | flight_id: any valid uuid | passenger_name: Test User

Test Steps:
1. POST /bookings with auth header and non-existent flight_id
2. Inspect response

1. GET /destinations/top
2. Inspect response
Expected Result: Status 200 | Response is a list | Each item has: city, iata_code, country, starting_price

Pre-condition: Cruises service is running on port 9002
Test Steps:
1. Send GET /health
2. Inspect response status and body

Pre-condition: Cruises service running; ports seeded in DB
Test Steps:
1. GET /ports
2. Inspect response
Expected Result: Status 200 | Response is a list | len >= 1 | Each item has: id, name, city, country

Test Steps:
1. GET /ports/search?q=Bar
2. Inspect response
Expected Result: Status 200 | List with >= 1 result | Each result has name/city field

Test Steps:
1. GET /ports/search?q=ZZZNOTEXIST
2. Inspect response

1. GET /ports/search (no q param)
2. Inspect response

1. GET /cruises/search (no params)
2. Inspect response shape
Expected Result: Status 200 | Response has keys: cruises, total

Test Steps:
1. GET /cruises/search?guests=2
2. Inspect each result

1. GET /cruises/search?date=2025-01-01
2. Inspect response shape only
Expected Result: Status 200 | Response has keys: cruises, total

Description: Get cruise by ID — not found

Pre-condition: Test user authenticated; seeded cruise with available_cabins >= 1
Test Steps:
1. GET /cruises/search?guests=1 (loop until total > 0)
2. Skip if none found
3. POST /cruise-bookings with auth and cruise_id
4. Inspect response
Test Data: Header: Authorization: Bearer <token> | cruise_id: <first result id> | passenger_name: ApiTest User | passenger_id_number: PASS123456 | date_of_birth: 1990-01-01 | num_guests: 1
Expected Result: Status 201 | booking_reference starts with 'LC' | status = 'confirmed' | cruise_id matches requested

Pre-condition: Test user authenticated; TC-CRU-010 has run
Test Steps:
1. GET /cruise-bookings/my with Authorization header
2. Inspect response

1. GET /cruise-bookings/{id} with Authorization header
2. Inspect response

1. GET /cruise-bookings/{id} with NO Authorization header
2. Inspect response

1. PATCH /cruise-bookings/{id}/cancel with Authorization header
2. Inspect response

1. PATCH /cruise-bookings/{id}/cancel again
2. Inspect response
Test Data: Header: Authorization: Bearer <token> | booking_id: (same as TC-CRU-014)

Test Steps:
1. POST /cruise-bookings with no Authorization header
2. Inspect response
Test Data: No Authorization header | cruise_id: any valid uuid

Description: Create cruise booking — invalid cruise ID
Test Steps:
1. POST /cruise-bookings with auth header and non-existent cruise_id
2. Inspect response

1. GET /destinations/top
2. Inspect response
Expected Result: Status 200 | Response is a list | Each item has: city, country, starting_price
